why_new: iteration 5 — CONVERGENT KERNEL, DISTINCT ENVELOPE. Same loop-around-stateless-model family as LangChain AgentExecutor / LlamaIndex AgentRunner. Differentiation lives ABOVE the kernel: 3-layer memory with cross-session user-fact extraction, eval-as-first-class (4 modes), AgentOS bundled runtime. Counterexample to the kernel-discriminator thesis. Old cold-instantiation benchmarks are misleading; v2 docs de-emphasized them.
next_followups: Verify 'convergent kernel' claim by reading AgentRunner source

query: AFlow paper deep-read with benchmark table extraction
why_new: iteration 8 — MAJOR CORRECTION. AFlow does NOT benchmark MetaGPT/AutoGen/ChatDev as named baselines (mentions: 1/0/0 times, all in references). Actual baselines: IO, CoT, CoT-SC 5-shot, MedPrompt, MultiPersona Debate, Self-Refine + ADAS. Headline: 80.3% avg vs CoT-SC 76.0% (+4.3 abs); DeepSeek-V2.5 running AFlow-searched workflow matches GPT-4o IO at 4.55% of cost on HumanEval. The cycle-7 synthesis '4 of 13 frameworks have benchmark scores' drops to 2 of 13 once AFlow is ruled out as a metagpt/chatdev source.
